notes.implement: Frontier GPT-5.6 model for complex coding and professional work.

notes.spec: Best GPT-5.6 option for complex structured spec drafting.
notes.research: Strong GPT-5.6 option for complex synthesis and technical research.

notes.spec_review: Balanced GPT-5.6 model for requirements review at lower cost than Sol.
notes.implement: Balanced GPT-5.6 model for general coding work and cost-aware implementation.
notes.review: Good GPT-5.6 review option when balancing depth and cost.
notes.spec: Balanced GPT-5.6 option for structured spec drafting.
notes.research: Balanced GPT-5.6 option for research synthesis and analysis.

notes.spec_review: Cost-sensitive GPT-5.6 option for quick spec checks.

notes.spec: Cost-sensitive GPT-5.6 option for simple spec drafting.
notes.research: Cost-sensitive GPT-5.6 option for targeted research and summaries.

notes.spec_review: Good spec reviewer; solid reasoning on requirements and edge cases.

notes.review: Thorough code reviewer; effective at catching logic issues.
notes.spec: Latest GPT-5 generation; strong at structured spec drafting.

notes.spec_review: Reliable spec reviewer; covers most issues in typical specs.
notes.implement: Good implementation quality; well-suited for mid-complexity features.
notes.review: Good code review; catches common patterns and obvious issues.
notes.spec: Reliable spec drafter; good balance of reasoning and speed.
notes.research: Strong research synthesis at GPT-5 tier; good at structured multi-source analysis.

notes.spec_review: Quick spec scan; may miss nuanced issues in complex specs.

notes.spec_review: Good for technical spec review; strong code reasoning.

notes.spec_review: Reliable spec review for standard complexity.

placeholders.AGENT_DEV_SERVER_NOTE:
> **CRITICAL — Codex dev server rules:**
>
> **NEVER run the project's configured dev command directly.** Running dev commands directly bypasses port allocation and will cause port conflicts.
>
> Always use `aigon dev-server start` for port allocation and proxy registration.
>
> Because Codex kills background processes when shell commands exit, use `--register-only` mode:
>
> 1. Run `aigon dev-server start --register-only` — allocates your agent's port and writes it to the project's port configuration
> 2. Read the allocated port from the project's port configuration (see worktree setup output)
> 3. Start the server in a **persistent terminal session** using `/terminal` with the allocated port and the project's configured dev command
> 4. Get your app URL: `aigon dev-server url`
> 5. Use that URL for all testing
